top_categories.workout_context: 18,045ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
At mile 60 of 141, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
128ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Otztaler Radmarathon, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 141 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Otztaler Radmarathon's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before August.
18,045ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Otztaler Radmarathon. Train it until it feels boring.
Otztaler Radmarathon's paved Alpine mountain passes forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 141 miles.
141 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Low-cadence force work for 18,045ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 18,045ft of climbing on paved Alpine mountain passes does to your power file on race day.

race_overlay.heat: August in Solden, Otztal Valley, Tyrol, Austria can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Otztaler Radmarathon. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Otztaler Radmarathon’s 141 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Otztaler Radmarathon with 18,045ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%. Timmelsjoch summit at 2,474m: wind chill can bring effective temperature below freezing; 30+ km of climbing to the Timmelsjoch means prolonged cold exposure at altitude.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Durability, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 141 miles of paved alpine mountain passes in Solden, Otztal Valley, Tyrol, Austria.
